growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: fierer.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Methylocapsa aurea mineral salts medium (NaNO3-based, Fe-NTA)

up.method: MIMS Vmax (accelerated from 4 to 30 over time)

sec.method: CO2 in gas outflow dropped after H2 supply (Table S4); not quantified per gDW

yields.Yxs: 2.8 g DW/mol CH4 (CH4 alone) -> 5.4 g DW/mol CH4 (with H2), measured at D=0.001 h-1

growth_records.cite: Ida F. Peterse et al. Hydrogen Oxidation Benefits Alphaproteobacterial Methanotrophs Under Severe Methane Limitation. Environmental Microbiology (2025). DOI:10.1111/1462-2920.70163. [PMC12319191]
growth_records.snippet: D = 0.002 h-1 for M. aurea KYG T... a similar experiment was performed with the CH4-limited bioreactor culture (D = 0.001 h-1). H2 consumption was instantaneous and 6- to 7-fold higher than CH4 consumption... we calculated a yield increase
growth_records.notes: CH4/O2-limited chemostat D=0.002 h-1 = mu (kinetics condition); td=ln2/0.002=346.6 h. Vmax CH4=93 umol/min/gDW (x0.06=5.58 mmol/gDW/h); Km(app) CH4=3.5 uM. Vmax H2 4->30 umol/min/gDW (0.24->1.80 mmol/gDW/h); Km(app) H2=0.04-0.10 uM. Culture verified contaminant-free. DW: OD600=1 = 205.8+/-5.6 mg/L (D=0.002). Yield increase 2.8->5.4 g DW/mol CH4 (with H2) was measured on a separate CH4-limited run at D=0.001 h-1 (slightly lower than kinetics D=0.002); H2 consumption 6-7x CH4 consumption. Rates are MIMS Vmax = uptake.
